methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $125M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Kyoto Fusioneering is a Series C nuclear energy company that has raised $125M+ to date. The nuclear energy sector comprises 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum sample thresholds—only 2 adjacent-stage peers were identified within the nuclear energy sector, falling short of the 3-datapoint floor required for reliable triangulation. Consequently, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C-to-next-stage advancement. Using the $125M total raised as a valuation proxy, this method yields $212M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $181M to $244M, centered on $212M. This range reflects uncertainty inherent in single-method triangulation and the nascent maturity of the fusion and advanced nuclear sector, where comparables remain sparse and stage economics remain unproven at scale. Key uncertainties include the absence of profitable precedent in the target's specific technology niche and reliance on stage-progression assumptions rather than peer multiples. Additional due diligence should address the company's capital efficiency, path to commercialization, and competitive positioning relative to the identified peer set.
